title: Uplifted Chinese Tahini Bao
description: A light, grassy bao with tahini broth, steamed in a wok, perfect for a sick day.
instructions: Mix the almond flour, coconut flour, xanthan gum and salt in a bowl.
Add the egg and water to the dry mixture and stir until a smooth dough forms.
Let the dough rest for 10 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal portions and roll each into a small ball.
Flatten each ball into a thin circle and place a spoonful of the tahini broth mixture in the center.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and seal the edges with a finger.
Heat a wok over medium-high heat and add a splash of sesame oil.
Place the bao in the wok and cover with a lid, steaming for 8 minutes.
Remove the lid and turn the bao over, steaming for an additional 4 minutes.
Serve hot with sliced scallions on top.
